title: dodonaea viscosa subf. laurina
intro: Dodonaea viscosa subf. laurina, also known as Sticky Hopbush, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its sticky stems and leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of Dodonaea viscosa subf. laurina can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and have been traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including wounds, burns, and respiratory issues.
